2605.05438.title: On Semantic Loss Fine-Tuning Approach for Preventing Model Collapse in Causal Reasoning
2605.05438.paper_summary: Standard fine-tuning of transformer models on causal reasoning tasks leads to catastrophic model collapse, where models learn trivial solutions such as always predicting "Yes" or "No" regardless of input structure. We demonstrate that fine-tuning Gemma 270M on transitivity and d-separation tasks without semantic loss results in 100% collapse rate, with models achieving misleadingly high accuracy (73.9%) while learning no causal reasoning. We propose a semantic loss function with graph-based logical constraints and dynamic lambda scheduling that prevents this collapse. Our approach achieves 70.4% accuracy on transitivity tasks and 68.6% on d-separation tasks with stable, context-dependent predictions, representing a 42.7% improvement over collapsed baselines. Adversarial evaluation on 1,000 structural reasoning samples shows semantic models achieve 67-70% accuracy while collapsed models fail catastrophically at 43-71%. We validate our findings through comprehensive benchmarking on 200,000+ evaluation samples across five model variants, demonstrating that semantic loss is essential and not optional, for stable causal reasoning in transformers.
2605.05438.paper_summary_zh: 標準的微調Transformer模型在因果推理任務上會導致災難性的模型崩潰，模型學會了平凡的解決方案，例如無論輸入結構如何，總是預測「是」或「否」。我們展示了在不損失語義的情況下，對Gemma 270M進行微調以處理傳遞性和d-分離任務會導致100%的崩潰率，模型在學習不到因果推理的情況下，卻達到了誤導性的高準確率（73.9%）。我們提出了一種具有基於圖的邏輯約束和動態lambda調度的語義損失函數，防止這種崩潰。我們的方法在傳遞性任務上達到了70.4%的準確率，在d-分離任務上達到了68.6%的準確率，並且預測穩定且依賴於上下文，這比崩潰的基準提高了42.7%。在1,000個結構推理樣本上的對抗性評估顯示，語義模型的準確率達到67-70%，而崩潰模型的準確率卻在43-71%之間災難性失敗。我們通過對五種模型變體在200,000+評估樣本上的全面基準測試來驗證我們的發現，證明語義損失對於Transformer中的穩定因果推理是必不可少的，而不是可選的。
2605.05438.author: Pratik Deshmukh et.al.
